topics: Various forms of natural rubber: crumb, sheet, crepe, SP rubber, DPNR, liquid rubber, powdered rubber
Curing characteristics such as scorch time, induction time, cure time
Mechanism of crosslinking by different crosslinking agents: sulphur, sulphur monochloride, phenolic resins, metal oxide, diamines, peroxides, oximes
Different curing systems: semi-EV, conventional and sulphur-less cure
Manufacture, structure, properties, processing, vulcanisation and applications of SBR, polybutadiene and polyisoprene rubber, and silicone rubbers

Monomer preparation, polymerization, structure, properties and applications of the following rubbers: chlorosulphonated rubbers, fluorine containing rubbers, acrylic rubbers, polysulphide rubber, polyalkenamers, polynorbornene, thermoplastic elastomers
